refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these obligations are separated. This refined FR focuses solely on the derivation of performance requirements, which is a distinct functional need independent of how the requirements are recorded or managed.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can analyze worksite mission context inputs and produce a documented set of performance requirements such as cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability without specifying how these are recorded or managed.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements.
The NGHE solution shall manage the history of performance requirements and calibration updates.

candidate_dps: Define a performance requirements derivation process that maps worksite mission inputs to performance targets
Establish a traceable documentation framework linking worksite inputs to performance requirements
Implement a baseline and calibration update management system to maintain history
Integrate safety plan and operational policy constraints into performance requirement derivation
selected_dp.selected_dp: Define a performance requirements derivation process that maps worksite mission inputs to performance targets

potential_sources: Integration of safety plan and operational policies may introduce dependencies if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical mapping process from mission context to performance targets, supporting independence. Other aspects like documentation and history management can be modularized separately to avoid coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes worksite mission inputs are well-defined and stable
Risk that safety and operational policies may change frequently, requiring flexible update mechanisms
Assumes documentation and history management are handled in separate modules to maintain DP independence
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating documentation and history management DPs to ensure modularity and reduce coupling with the derivation process DP.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system responsible for processing mission inputs and deriving performance targets, integrating multiple subsystems including Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission input data and operational status from the worksite.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission task assignments and parameters.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts related to performance targets impacting safety.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the performance requirements derivation process is central to the overall solution; minimal diffusion as most related data and control flows are internal.
Partial containment since vehicle performance is a subset, but derivation process spans beyond vehicle specifics causing diffusion.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control, not performance target derivation, leading to high diffusion.
Moderate containment as safety targets relate to performance, but derivation process is broader than safety alone.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the performance requirements derivation process because it integrates inputs from multiple subsystems and produces system-wide performance targets, ensuring high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity within NGHE Solution may require clear internal modularization to avoid internal diffusion.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Safety Services must be well-defined to prevent leakage of derivation logic outside the boundary.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP clearly defines a process that maps the worksite mission inputs to performance targets, directly addressing the FR requirement to derive performance requirements from the mission context. The DP is logically consistent and traceable to the FR.

diffusion_risks: Interfaces with Vehicle and Safety Services modules show some diffusion risk as derivation logic may leak outside the NGHE Solution boundary.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 1), indicating good packaging of the DP within a single IM. However, internal complexity may require further modularization to maintain low diffusion, especially at interfaces with Vehicle and Safety Services.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives mission inputs via Telemetry and Tasking interfaces and produces documented performance targets traceable to inputs, fulfilling acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear since the NGHE Solution module is the designated IM boundary responsible for the DP, integrating multiple subsystems and managing inputs and outputs relevant to performance derivation.

recommended_revisions: Introduce clear internal modularization within the NGHE Solution module to manage complexity and prevent internal diffusion of the derivation process.
Define and document precise interface contracts with Vehicle and Safety Services modules to prevent leakage of derivation logic and maintain boundary integrity.

Ensure traceability mechanisms are implemented within the NGHE Solution module to link performance targets back to worksite mission inputs and maintain history of baselines/calibration updates as per acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: Although the current FR, DP, and IM mappings pass all gates with clear traceability and verification feasibility, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks at interfaces with Vehicle and Safety Services modules necessitate further decomposition. Introducing next-level sub-FRs will enable clearer internal modularization within the NGHE Solution module, better manage complexity, and define precise interface contracts to mitigate diffusion risks and maintain boundary integrity.
next_level_sub_frs: Derive performance targets from telemetry inputs
Manage interface with Safety Services module

revise.why: To introduce clear internal modularization within the NGHE Solution module to manage complexity and prevent internal diffusion of the derivation process, and to define precise interface contracts with Vehicle and Safety Services modules.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is currently a single IM with high containment but internal complexity.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Safety Services modules are critical and pose diffusion risks.
open_risks: Potential leakage of derivation logic outside NGHE Solution boundary at interfaces.
Internal complexity may lead to diffusion if not modularized.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specifications with Vehicle and Safety Services modules.
Modularization plan within NGHE Solution module.
Traceability documentation linking performance targets to mission inputs.
